behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted file paths, test names (DragonboxMSan/ReturnValuePaddingShadowIsLost), literal strings (BOOST_USE_MSAN, MEMORY_SANITIZER, struct padding, per-field/LLVM IR), and structural requirements are named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and what to document, without giving exact line numbers or replacement code.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for substring presence and general structural requirements that any correct fix would include.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_cmake_boost_use_msan_defined, test_fiber_h_comment_updated, test_fiber_stack_cpp_comment_explains_msan_unpoison, and test_dragonbox_msan_test_file_exists all just grep file contents; no code is compiled or executed to verify the MSan behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: The tests require specific strings and file structures that an empty stub would not provide.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each required change (CMake MSAN, Fiber.h comment, FiberStack.cpp comment, new test file) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings explaining each check.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers match between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to relevant files but doesn't leak specific line numbers; file identification is part of the stated scope.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The CMake edit is a small elseif addition, localized to the sanitizer section.
